constraints: All nine gates must pass before a packet executes; partial execution is forbidden ('does not execute a little bit') — p0-pgr/P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md.
Cloud-only: MAC_RUNNER and HYBRID_MAC are forbidden routes, including as fallback; the Mac is cockpit, never runner — p0-pgr/P0_DISPATCH_ROUTER_RULES_v1.md.

FOUNDER_ONLY, always: deploy, merge, L5 verifier changes, phase unlocks, authority changes, scheduled automation approval, retiring a founder trigger — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md. A cron/24-7 factory loop therefore needs explicit founder approval; scorecard currently records ladder_r3 'HOLD — cron disabled'.

no_deploy gate + HARD_BLOCK reason 2: no production deploy/promote — the factory's deploy line must be preview-only unless founder-authorized per deployment (precedent: TEAM_BENCH_PLATFORM_PROD_DEPLOY_v1 counted in scorecard deploys with founder authorization).
no_external_send gate + HARD_BLOCK reason 1: nothing sent to humans outside the repo — client-facing delivery from factory lines is gated.

founder_authorization_receipt gate: an in-repo receipt must exist in receipts/p0pgr/founder/ before acting; chat-only authorization is not durable evidence (M03 lesson) — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md.

Continuity law: never default to STOP; degrade tag confidence -> reduce scope -> sandbox -> partial -> provisional -> retry-if-ROI-positive -> review queue; lint failure files a repair candidate and the lane keeps moving — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md / P0_PROMPT_COMPILER_MVP_PLAN_v1.md.

Lint R7: packets may not route validation/review/uncertainty to the founder (violates autonomy loops); R6 bans vague-verb missions without measurable acceptance — P0_PROMPT_COMPILER_MVP_PLAN_v1.md, scripts/p0pgr_packet_lint_v1.py.

Receipt provenance: timestamps machine-generated at event time (datetime.now(timezone.utc)); every external claim needs per-URL status + body sha256 + saved body under receipts/p0pgr/artifacts/<receipt-id>/; route deviations must be recorded in executor_route_note — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md.

P0 leakage wall (gate no_p0_leakage): workers/prompt bundles may never contain P0 CORE reasoning; worker task specs restricted to the seven allowed keys; forbidden_readers include worker/normal_worker/tier_1_worker/tier_2_prompt_bundle — data/p0_core_decision_use_contract_v1.json.
SESSION_EMBEDDED rail (INCIDENT-039): max one light shell <=90s per turn on the Mac; heavy work routes to cloud — P0_DISPATCH_ROUTER_RULES_v1.md.
Phase 3 (earned autonomy) is LOCKED — founder unlock only; M4 cloud execution LOCKED until first gated packet, M5 LOCKED — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md, P0_PROMPT_COMPILER_MVP_PLAN_v1.md.
Integration law (census F3): consume adjacent live systems as inputs, never duplicate their queues — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md.
Fixed report format: 'OBSERVED · <operation sections> · QUALITY STATE · RECEIPT · SCORECARD · NEXT · STOP' — P0_DISPATCH_BRAIN_RUNTIME_v1.1.md.
reusable_vocabulary: Schema consts: p0_prompt_packet_v1.1, p0_execution_receipt_v1, p0_prompt_loop_state_v1, p0pgr_phase2_queue_v1, p0pgr_phase2_scorecard_v1, p0pgr_repair_candidate_v1, p0pgr_evidence_digest_v1, p0_core_decision_use_contract_v1
packet_id pattern: ^PKT-[0-9]{4}(-[a-z0-9-]+)?$ (e.g. PKT-0002-noetfield-live-truth, PKT-0101-runtime-health-revalidate)
Routes: CLOUD_WORKER, CLOUD_RESEARCH, SESSION_EMBEDDED, REVIEW_QUEUE; forbidden: MAC_RUNNER, HYBRID_MAC; label: HOLD_CLOUD_UNSAFE
Nine gate keys: cloud_only, read_only_or_reversible, roi_positive, no_deploy, no_external_send, no_legal_financial_commitment, no_p0_leakage, no_authority_change, founder_authorization_receipt

Packet statuses: DRAFT, OUTBOX, ROUTED, HOLD_CLOUD_UNSAFE, QUEUED_FOUNDER_REVIEW, DONE, KILLED; cycle verdicts: ROUTED, REPAIR_CANDIDATE, HOLD_CLOUD_UNSAFE, QUEUED_FOUNDER_REVIEW; quality_state: PASS, PARTIAL, PROVISIONAL, FAIL; confidence: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW
ROI fields + weights: revenue 35, trust 25, workload_relief 15, cloud_now 15, reversibility 10 (each scored 0-5); queue field next_move
Evidence kinds: url_fetch, script_run, file_stat, hash; evidence item fields: claim, kind, url, status, sha256, saved_path

Receipt field names: receipt_id, recorded_at, gates_checked, quality_state, limitations, evidence, cost{llm_calls, metered_note}, accounting_note, executor_route_note, findings, commit_flag; loop-state: cycle_id, packet_path, stages{compiled,linted,routed}, lint_rejects, gate_results, counters{executions,sends,deploys,leaks,freezes}

Lint codes R1-R9; repair candidate filing pattern repair-<packet_id>-<YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ>.json; loop-state filing pattern loop-state-cycle-<ts>.json; execution receipt pattern execution-<PKT-id>-<ts>.json; compact timestamp format %Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ

Era/phase names: PHASE_2_CLOUD_ONLY_ROI_TRACK; MVP step ids M0-M5; ladder ids ladder_r2/ladder_r3; six board axes: runtime_health, authority_wiring, commercial_readiness, live_truth, cost_roi, delivery_readiness; CHESS pass: forecast, patch, proceed, verify
Directory layout under receipts/p0pgr/: outbox/, founder/, artifacts/, campaigns/, repair_candidates/, phase2_queue_v1.json, phase2_scorecard_v1.json, phase1_scorecard_v1.json

P0 CORE wall vocabulary: read_only_judgment_source, output_soup_wall, worker_task_spec_allowed_keys (goal, files, constraints, done_criteria, verify_method, receipts_required, decision_verdict), allowed_readers base_live_brain/high_decision_agent, stop_continue_verdict STOP|CONTINUE|ESCALATE_TO_SG, harvest_proposed

Deliverable types already in use: truth_diff_report, validation_report, commit_manifest; labels in use: SUPPORTING_TRUST_PROOF, live_truth; source_findings convention: census F1/F3; lesson ids: M03, INCIDENT-039
gaps: No factory-line taxonomy: the route enum has only 4 coarse routes (CLOUD_WORKER, CLOUD_RESEARCH, SESSION_EMBEDDED, REVIEW_QUEUE). There is no field for the requested line types (research, architecture, code, no-code app builder, workflow builder, test, deploy-preview, verifier, receipt, harvest); a substrate must either add a line field (e.g. reuse labels[] or a new deliverable.type convention) or extend the enum, which requires synchronized changes to P0_PROMPT_PACKET_SCHEMA_v1.json, p0pgr_packet_lint_v1.py ALLOWED_ROUTES, and P0_DISPATCH_ROUTER_RULES_v1.md — and likely FOUNDER_ONLY authority sign-off.

No executor-binding field in the packet: nothing names a concrete execution target (GitHub Actions workflow, Cloudflare Worker, Railway API, Supabase, repo worktree/sandbox id). route is only a class; executor_route_note in receipts is free text after the fact.

No automated executor exists: scripts/p0pgr_cycle_v1.py ends at verdict ROUTED and never invokes anything (M4 is LOCKED in the MVP plan). The 8 executions on the scorecard were manual founder-authorized passes (e.g. receipts/p0pgr/P0PGR_R2_CLOUD_MANUAL_PASS_v1-20260709T001540Z.json). The substrate's dispatch layer from ROUTED -> sandbox is entirely missing.

founder_authorization_receipt gate is coarse: founder_receipt_exists() in p0pgr_cycle_v1.py passes if ANY *.json exists in receipts/p0pgr/founder/ — there is no per-packet or per-scope authorization binding.

No compiler script: the 'compiled' stage is set true unconditionally in run_cycle(); packet compilation is operator/skill work (anthropic-skills:p0pgr-packet-compiler), not code — a substrate turning founder/client orders into packets automatically has no existing script to extend.

No queue-consumption loop: phase2_queue_v1.json exposes next_move but nothing consumes it; scheduled automation is FOUNDER_ONLY and ladder_r3 is 'HOLD — cron disabled', so a 24/7 factory motor needs both new code and a founder unlock receipt.
No packet status lifecycle enforcement: nothing programmatically transitions status OUTBOX -> ROUTED -> DONE/KILLED after execution; outbox packets are hand-edited (PKT-0002 is status DONE in place).

No preview-artifact schema: deliverable.destination is a repo path string; there is no schema for hosted preview URLs (Cloudflare/Railway/Supabase) or client-visible artifacts, and gate no_external_send currently forbids delivery to humans outside the repo.

No independent verifier line: quality_state in execution receipts is self-reported; the schema-revalidation verifier was only a proposed campaign candidate (PKT-0101-runtime-health-revalidate). loop-state gate_results is an unconstrained object (no required keys).

No harvest schema in P0-PGR: harvest_proposed/harvest_note exist only in the P0 CORE decision contract output schema (data/p0_core_decision_use_contract_v1.json), not in packet or execution receipt schemas — a harvest line needs a new receipt shape.

Cost tracking is minimal: cost = {llm_calls int, metered_note string}; scorecard cost_usd is 0 with no accumulation mechanism — no per-line or per-sandbox spend attribution for Railway/Cloudflare/Supabase.

Minor doc/code divergence to reconcile: route_packet() exempts SESSION_EMBEDDED packets from the cloud_only -> HOLD_CLOUD_UNSAFE rule (scripts/p0pgr_cycle_v1.py line 30), an exception not written in P0_DISPATCH_ROUTER_RULES_v1.md.

constraints: "Mac executes nothing on schedule; every line writes a receipt; founder approvals are structural API gates, not conventions" — rule field of data/cloud_line_registry_v1.json and plan §2 (docs/CLOUD_FACTORY_PROMOTION_PLAN_TRUSTFIELD_v1.md).
No receipt sink = no loop (governed-autorun L8/L11 cited as P0's prerequisite) — docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md.

DECLARED until 24h zero-manual window of scheduled externally-written receipts closes green, then VERIFIED; manual green != cron green — docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md doctrine header.

Every p0pgr execution receipt must pass 9 gates: cloud_only, read_only_or_reversible, roi_positive, no_deploy, no_external_send, no_legal_financial_commitment, no_p0_leakage, no_authority_change, founder_authorization_receipt — receipts/p0pgr/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_ACTIVATION_v1-20260709T011208Z.json and trustfield-cloud-loops-built-v1-20260709T001150Z.json.
Structural guards on all packets: no candidate contact, no email send, no public copy change; email defer stays ON until founder flips it — docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md.
Drift -> deploy_truth_hold row blocks L3/L4 autoruns until founder resolves; hold clearance is a founder point — data/cloud_line_registry_v1.json L6 + packets P3/P5.

No unregistered/inactive prompt_id may run in cloud (runtime refusal gate); contract-test failure blocks only new version, previous version keeps running; two versions never active at once; cost_class dollars requires founder-activated budget line / L8 approval — plan §4 + data/prompt_registry_schema_v1.json + packet P7.
L8 never auto-approves; L1 never auto-rollbacks; every founder decision writes an approval_decision receipt with verbatim scope (M03 lesson) — data/cloud_line_registry_v1.json + plan §1.

Verification by external runner only, >=60s post-deploy (law L4); redirect policy OFF + full-body marker checks (D8); LLM output is a proposal, validator gates persistence (D7); same op_key twice -> one row (D1) — docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md.
Registry is authority over implementation orders (recorded conflict resolution: 6h vs */30 -> "Registry wins") — docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md.
R3 cron for the SSOT P0-PGR brain remains locked (separate ladder, untouched) — activation receipt known_conditions.

The four architecture artifacts are sha256-pinned in receipts/p0pgr/CLOUD_FACTORY_PROMOTION_PLAN_TRUSTFIELD_v1-20260708T234714Z.json — in-place edits to cloud_line_registry_v1.json invalidate a founder-receipted package.
A ladder step is DONE only when its receipt exists; no step contacts anyone, sends email, or publishes public UX — docs/ACTIVATION_LADDER_v1.md.
reusable_vocabulary: line_id: L1..L8; line names: Platform Health Line, Candidate Intake Line, Team Bench Sourcing Line, Agentic Evaluation Line, Prompt Registry Line, Deploy Truth Line, Asset Gate Line, Founder Approval Queue
owner_role values: noos_runtime, platform, sourcing_agent, evaluation_agent, architect, verifier, founder
trigger values: schedule, event, event+schedule, schedule+dispatch; run_trigger.kind enum: cron | event | manual

execution_target enum-like strings: github_actions, cloudflare_deadman_worker, railway_api, github_actions_nightly, claude_cloud_scheduled_agent, github_actions_runner, railway_api_deterministic, github_actions_llm_narrative, github_registry_ci, railway_serving_prompt_runs, cloudflare_verify_recipes_worker, railway_asset_views_logging, railway_api_admin_dashboard, github_actions_telegram_digest

receipt_type values on ops_receipts: health_check, intake_summary, sourcing_run, evaluation_run, deploy_truth, deploy_truth_hold, asset_gate_audit, approval_decision, prompt_registry_seeded, receipt_summary, cloud_factory_tables_live; receipt class TEAM_BENCH_SOURCED_ROWS
prompt_id convention: lane-prefixed kebab, pattern ^(tb|pa|ops)\.[a-z0-9-]+\.[a-z0-9-]+$ — e.g. tb.sourcing.r1/r3/r4, pa.eval.review-pack, ops.health.narrative

prompt registry field names: prompt_id, version (semver, immutable once activated), lane, owner (architect|founder|machine), prompt_body_ref, input_contract, output_schema, model_tier (determine|fast|standard|judgment), cost_class (zero|cents|dollars), run_trigger, receipt_path, rollback_rule, status (draft|testing|active|retired), founder_approval_required_to_activate, contract_tests_ref
state vocabulary: DECLARED, DECLARED_ACTIVE, VERIFIED; verdicts PASS / PARTIAL / PLATFORM_PACKAGE_PASS; BLOCKED_WITH_REASON; HOLD; SEV-1; IDLE/finding (zero-row run)

receipt schema names: p0pgr_execution_receipt_v1, p0pgr_architecture_package_v1, cloud_line_registry_v1, prompt_registry_schema_v1; receipt fields: receipt_id, recorded_at, packet_id, authorized_by, gates_checked, gates_note, quality_state, verdict, cost{llm_calls, metered_note}, accounting_note, executor_route_note, commit_flag, op_key, value_class
gate names (9): cloud_only, read_only_or_reversible, roi_positive, no_deploy, no_external_send, no_legal_financial_commitment, no_p0_leakage, no_authority_change, founder_authorization_receipt
law citations: governed-autorun L1-L13, deterministic core D1-D8 (D1 idempotency, D7 validator-gated persistence, D8 redirect-off checks, L4 external verification, L11 cost metering, L12 drift check)
era tag: PHASE_2_CLOUD_ONLY_ROI_TRACK; packet ids: P0 (foundation), P1..P7 mapping to lines L1,L2,L3,L4,L6,L7,L5

file naming: <PACKET_ID>-<ISO8601Z>.json under receipts/p0pgr/; workflow names pa-health-cron / pa-deploy-truth / pa-intake-nightly / pa-eval-nightly / pa-asset-gate-weekly / tb-sourcing-weekly / prompt-registry-ci; migration id 20260709_0006_cloud_factory_tables; scripts *_v1.py/.sh; docs *_v1.md; data *_v1.json
ladder steps A-F with per-step Preconditions/Artifacts/Verification/Receipt/Founder point structure (docs/ACTIVATION_LADDER_v1.md)
gaps: L8 Founder Approval Queue is NOT built: approval_queue table exists (migration 20260709_0006) but the GET/POST /api/ops/approvals endpoints, admin dashboard section, and daily Telegram digest (ladder step D) were explicitly out of scope per Loop Specialist order. The substrate's founder-approval routing has no live queue surface to plug into yet.
No loop is VERIFIED — everything is DECLARED_ACTIVE pending the 24h zero-manual window of scheduled receipts (activation receipt: verified_criteria_pending).

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not in GH Actions secrets — L3 sourcing and L4 evaluation LLM layers are dormant (L4 runs deterministic-only PARTIAL). The substrate's LLM-executing lines cannot assume a wired cloud LLM credential.

tb-sourcing-weekly primary (Claude Code cloud scheduled routine) was deliberately not created — only a workflow_dispatch fallback exists. There is no proven scheduled-cloud-agent execution path yet, only GH Actions crons.

L1-L8 cover operations loops for one venture only. None of the substrate's proposed factory stages exist as lines: research, architecture, code, no-code app builder, workflow builder, test, deploy-preview, verifier-as-line, harvest. There is also no line concept for isolated sandboxes/worktrees, per-order execution, or preview artifacts.
No client/founder ORDER intake exists — L2 intake is candidate application intake for TrustField, not order-to-packet compilation. The substrate needs a new order->prompt-packet front end.

The lane enum in data/prompt_registry_schema_v1.json is hardcoded to ["L1".."L8"] and prompt_id prefixes to (tb|pa|ops) — new factory lines require a schema v2 (or a parallel substrate schema) before any substrate prompt can be registered.

Extending cloud_line_registry_v1.json in place would break the sha256-pinned architecture package receipt and mix venture-scoped ops lines with venture-generic factory lines; the evidence points to a NEW registry file (e.g. a factory/substrate line registry reusing the identical record shape and rule string) with L1-L8 left intact and cross-referenced.

Supabase appears only as backing storage via Railway (auth, Postgres); no line executes on Supabase directly, and Cloudflare Workers are secondary/edge-check only — no Workers-primary execution pattern exists to copy.
Receipt dashboard (ladder step F, ops_receipts.receipt_summary) not built — no unified founder-visible view over cloud receipts.
First deploy-truth run was expected to open a HOLD (www worker at 46d394a vs main 164337d) requiring founder clearance — an open condition any new line depending on the HOLD contract inherits.

The 'deadman worker alarms if Actions goes silent' success criterion (plan §7) references an existing CF deadman worker pattern, but no receipt in these files proves the deadman path was wired for the new pa-health-cron.

constraints: No motor reports DONE without `receipt_id` — agent summaries are claims, not proof (loops JSON L7 `law` citing ssot/PARALLEL_AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE_v1.md L5; also loops MD agent rule 2).
Adversarial PASS only from a secondary CF account edge receipt — never builder self-report (L3-ADVERSARIAL `pass_rule`, data/machine_autonomy_loops_v1.json).

A loop is DECLARED at merge and VERIFIED only after a 24h zero-manual window of externally-written scheduled receipts with sink invariants + cost tables (L11) and drift check (L12) present; manual green ≠ cron green (docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md).

Founder involvement restricted to FT-CAPITAL, FT-LEGAL, FT-L5-IRREVERSIBLE, FT-PHASE-UNLOCK; hygiene failures must go through L4 self-repair then L6 research, never to founder (data/machine_autonomy_loops_v1.json founder_triggers_minimal; ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md §4–5; cycle receipt hardcodes founder_required: false).

FT-L5-IRREVERSIBLE never retires: prod promote without verifier PASS, force-push main, and disabling/weakening the promotion gate are forbidden by default — machine may only propose (data/machine_autonomy_loops_v1.json; agent rule 3: fix verifier path instead of weakening gate).

Autonomy expands only on receipt streaks per L8 expansion_rules (3× verifier + 3× drift PASS + gate ALIGNED for promote-candidate; 5× trap PASS + b2_signed + cost_per_signal < 0.01 for phase unlock; workflow_audit_pass + no_parallel_conflict for motor registration) — never on chat approval (ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md §6).

Self-repair budget: max 3 actions and 90s wall time per cycle; escalate when the same blocker persists 3 cycles or budget exhausted (L4 `repair_budget`/`escalate_when`); L6 research max_depth 2 hops, then freeze writes and open assist_only PR.

One writer per task cell, lane declaration before action, assist ≠ authority, registry is routing truth (ssot/PARALLEL_AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE_v1.md laws L1–L4); merges without lane + motor_id are an anti-pattern.

Mac executes nothing on schedule for cloud factory lines — crons live only in GH Actions/CF/Railway (docs/CLOUD_FACTORY_PROMOTION_PLAN_TRUSTFIELD_v1.md §2); Mac founder session runs L2 subset only per INCIDENT-039, full L3/L5 in cloud GH (ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md agent rule 5).

Ladder step discipline: preconditions → artifacts → verification → receipt; a step is DONE only when its receipt exists; no ladder step contacts anyone, sends email, or publishes public UX (docs/ACTIVATION_LADDER_v1.md header rule).

Structural (API-enforced, not conventional) guards: team bench admin create hardcodes status="sourced" (TrustField app/routers/team_bench.py:245); `approve_to_contact`/`mark_contacted` founder-gated; email defer stays ON; `cost_class: dollars` prompts require founder-activated budget line; prompt version activates only after CI contract tests pass and two versions are never active at once (rollback_rule).

24/7 autonomous deploy requires founder DECIDE plus `~/.sina/brain-autonomous-deploy-v1.flag`; any fail sets `brain-autonomous-hold-v1.flag` (auto-stop), cleared only on matrix ALL PASS (docs/PHASE3_FULL_AUTO_24x7_PLAN_v0.1.md Steps 5, 9, 10).
Proposals introducing any new founder trigger must state which loop retires it (ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md agent rule 4).
Default question gate for all new motors/escalations: "How does the process solve this without Sina?" — if the answer is 'ask the founder to check', the design is incomplete.
reusable_vocabulary: Loop IDs: `L1-WORKER-EXEC`, `L2-MACHINE-VALID`, `L3-ADVERSARIAL`, `L4-SELF-REPAIR`, `L5-OUTSIDE-AUDIT`, `L6-DEEP-RESEARCH`, `L7-RECEIPT-PROOF`, `L8-EARNED-AUTONOMY`; loop names: worker_execution, machine_validation, adversarial_critique, self_repair, outside_audit_advisory, deep_research_uncertainty, receipt_based_proof, earned_autonomy_expansion.
Founder trigger IDs: `FT-CAPITAL`, `FT-LEGAL`, `FT-L5-IRREVERSIBLE`, `FT-PHASE-UNLOCK` (classes capital_commitment, legal_regulatory, irreversible_l5, phase_unlock; each with `retire_when`).

Loop-object field names: loop_id, name, purpose, owner_class, motors, scripts, entry, exit_receipt, founder_required, on_failure, pass_rule, repair_budget, escalate_when, trigger_conditions, max_depth, expansion_rules ({from, to, requires}), cycle_order, anti_patterns, default_question.
Owner classes: venture_worker, sg_governance, sg_verifier, sg_verifier + venture_tests, sg_governance + noos_self_heal, noos_researcher + sg_observe, all_motors.

Autonomy states (L8): `observe_only`, `autonomous_promote_candidate`, `phase_N`/`phase_N_plus_1`, `assist_only`, `registered_motor`; streak keys: verifier_pass_streak, drift_pass_streak, gate_alignment ("ALIGNED"), trap_battery_pass_streak, b2_signed, cost_per_signal_below, workflow_audit_pass, no_parallel_conflict.

Motor IDs (registry naming convention `<platform>_<name>_v1`): cf_auto_runtime_tick_v1, cf_loop_specialist_tick_v1, gh_actions_noos_factory_autorun_v1, gh_actions_brain_loop_autorun_v1, mac_launchd_brain_loop_autorun_v1, gh_actions_noos_self_heal_loop_v1, gh_actions_noos_researcher_v1, gh_actions_noos_sourcea_observe_v1, sina-governance-ssot-advisory.

Receipt conventions: schema `machine-autonomy-cycle-v1`; receipt_id `machine-autonomy-cycle-<YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ>` (utc strftime %Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ); escalation enum values "L6-DEEP-RESEARCH" / "L4-SELF-REPAIR" / "none"; glob patterns receipts/automation-surface-audit-*.json, receipts/parallel-candidate-batch-*.json, receipts/brain-self-heal-tick-*.json, receipts/earned-autonomy-expansion-*.json, receipts/verifier-independence-proof-latest.json; SSOT mirror pattern receipts/trustfield-cloud-loop-<packet>-v1-<ISO8601Z>.json.

ops_receipts `receipt_type` values (TrustField): health_check, deploy_truth, evaluation_run, sourcing_run, asset_gate_audit, approval_decision, prompt_run, receipt_summary, intake_summary, prompt_registry_seeded, cloud_factory_tables_live; dedup key `op_key`.

Activation ladder step letters A–F; ladder step verdict word `PLATFORM_PACKAGE_PASS`; migration id `20260709_0006_cloud_factory_tables`; cloud line IDs L1–L8 (Health, Intake, Sourcing, Evaluation, Prompt Registry, Deploy Truth, Asset Gate, Approval Queue); implementation packet IDs P0–P7.

Prompt registry vocabulary: prompt_id lane-prefixed kebab (`tb.sourcing.r1/r3/r4`, `pa.eval.review-pack`, `ops.health.narrative`), model_tier enum determine/fast/standard/judgment, cost_class enum zero/cents/dollars, prompt status `testing`, rollback_rule, input_contract, output_schema, run_trigger.

Law citations: "PARALLEL_AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE L5" (receipt or unverified), governed-autorun L1–L13, deterministic core D1–D8; cycle verdict words: OK / DEGRADED (orchestrator), PASS / FAIL / PARTIAL / BLOCKED / DECLARED / VERIFIED / HOLD (loops and lines); flag files `brain-autonomous-deploy-v1.flag`, `brain-autonomous-hold-v1.flag`.
gaps: No R1/R2/R3 "rung" terminology exists anywhere in the repo (grep for 'rung' across docs/, ssot/, data/ returns nothing) — the activation ladder uses lettered steps A–F, and the r1/r3/r4 in `tb.sourcing.r1/r3/r4` are team-bench ROLE lanes, not ladder rungs. A Cloud Factory Substrate design must either adopt the existing A–F step convention or explicitly define a new rung vocabulary; it should not assume R-numbered rungs already exist.

No loop covers multi-line factory execution in isolated cloud sandboxes/worktrees: L1-WORKER-EXEC only covers "venture worker builds scoped artifact in own repo"; the only worktree machinery is the repair script scripts/repair_sourcea_worktree_v1.sh. Substrate needs a sandbox/worktree provisioning + isolation loop (or an L1 extension) with its own motors, entry contract, and exit_receipt.

PASS/HOLD is not a formalized enum in data/machine_autonomy_loops_v1.json — HOLD exists only as scattered mechanisms (~/.sina/brain-autonomous-hold-v1.flag, TrustField deploy_truth HOLD flag rows, prompt-activation blocks). The substrate would need one unified hold/verdict representation consumable by all execution targets.

scripts/run_machine_autonomy_cycle_v1.py hardcodes its L2 validator list (lines 45–57) instead of reading the `scripts` arrays already present in the loops JSON — plugging substrate validators into the cycle currently requires editing the orchestrator, not just the registry.

No factory-line stage vocabulary (research, architecture, code, no-code app builder, workflow builder, test, deploy-preview, verifier, receipt, harvest) exists in the autonomy loops — the closest is the TrustField cloud line map L1–L8, which is venture-specific (candidates/sourcing), not a generic order→packet→line pipeline. The substrate must define generic line IDs without colliding with the three existing L-number namespaces (autonomy loops L1–L8, cloud lines L1–L8, governance laws L1–L5 / governed-autorun L1–L13 / deterministic core D1–D8) — a disambiguating prefix is effectively mandatory.

Retirement backlog items still open (ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md §8): verifier deploy via `wrangler deploy` in CI post-merge ("L4 — add GH workflow step") and promote-gate-by-verifier-PASS-streak ("L3/L5 in progress") — the substrate's deploy-preview/verifier lines would land on these unfinished paths.

Activation ladder steps B–F are all unstarted as of 2026-07-08 (only A is DONE): the prompt_registry/prompt_runs/approval_queue/ops_receipts/asset_views tables, the GH Actions loops, the founder approval queue endpoints, and the receipt dashboard the substrate would depend on do not exist yet — they are planned in migration `20260709_0006_cloud_factory_tables` and packets P0–P7.

No cost/ROI attribution schema for factory-line runs beyond the prompt registry's cost_class and the weekly write_roi_heartbeat_v1.py — per-order or per-packet cost roll-up (needed for FT-CAPITAL retire_when "30d spend under cap with receipts") is not defined.

docs/PHASE3_FULL_AUTO_24x7_PLAN_v0.1.md is brain-loop-specific (Mac launchd + SourceA); its Step 10 founder-flag pattern is the only codified 24/7-enable mechanism — there is no equivalent enable/hold flag design for cloud-hosted substrate lines (GH Actions/CF/Railway), and Phase 4 items ("Cloud Forge autorun") are explicitly out of scope there.

constraints: No direct main writes: canon §5 wall 'main writes'; fences enforce only on enforce_target_branches: [main]; protected-path merge to main requires receipt (RF_RECEIPT_REQUIRED) — source policy/repo_fences_v1.yaml + scripts/check_repo_fences_v1.py.

Policy/law mutation gate: any change matching policy_law_globs (ssot/**/*.md, policy/**/*.yaml|yml, packages/noos-control-desk-v1 LAW/COPILOT_/NOOS_/SMART_ files) merged to main requires work_order_id AND receipt (RF_POLICY_LAW_MUTATION) — policy/repo_fences_v1.yaml.

No fake ACTIVE: while package_status is audit-pending, promotions to ACTIVE are blocked by RF1_package_status_active / RF2_operational_rollout_active / RF3_status_flip_from_audit_pending — policy/repo_fences_v1.yaml; substrate lines must never self-promote status.
Agents never produce approvals, sign-offs, status promotions, or ledger entries — only evidence; missing authority → BLOCKED_WITH_REASON, never improvisation — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §4.

Receipts required: no DONE without receipt_id (MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS §7 rule 2; PARALLEL_AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE L5); every receipt carries canon_version founder_canon_v1.0.0 — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md dispatch line + data/founder_canon_v1.json receipt_required_fields.

Mechanical walls at sandbox boundary (G1-G8 classes): main writes, governance/SSOT/ledger writes, approval language, token handling, hidden state, global installs, deploys, destructive git, out-of-scope files — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §5; the substrate must implement these as walls that fire on escape, not permission loops that fire on normal work (intent filter Q3).

Failures route to machines, not the founder: detect→contain→critique→audit→deep_research→repair→validate→receipt→continue_or_escalate; founder reached only via FT-CAPITAL / FT-LEGAL / FT-L5-IRREVERSIBLE / FT-PHASE-UNLOCK — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §6-§8, ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md §4-§5.

Never weaken a verifier, gate, law, or schema (FT-L5-IRREVERSIBLE, never auto-retire; 'Never weaken L5 promotion gate — fix verifier path') — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §8, ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md §7.

One writer per task cell; second writer gets REJECTED receipt; every actor declares lane + motor_id before editing; unregistered automation is a defect — ssot/PARALLEL_AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE_v1.md L1/L2/L4.

Assist ≠ authority: Copilot/Coding Agents default assist_only and may never promote brain, deploy prod, register artifacts, append SG/NOOS canonical, or external send; GitHub Actions forbidden from prod deploys / auto-merge to main without promotion gate unless registered — ssot/PARALLEL_AUTOMATION_GOVERNANCE_v1.md L3 §5-§6.

Approval before external send: 'No external message leaves any TrustField loop without human approval in v1'; regulated inbound (custody, settlement, MSB, PSP, banking) always routes to Sina/legal; risk_score >= 4 → route — ssot/sg-guardrails-trustfield-v1.md, ssot/sg-guardrails-signal-factory-v1.md.

No legal/equity/payment/employment claims: no regulated-capability claims unless legally established; partner/equity signals → bounded exploratory action only, no commitment language; no employment implication; claim ladder VERIFIED/DECLARED/PLANNED with no evidence-free upgrades — ssot/sg-guardrails-trustfield-v1.md guardrails 1/3/4/5.

Autonomy expansion only on receipt streaks (3x verifier PASS etc.), never chat approval; preview/observe before promote (observe_only → promote_candidate → phase unlock ladder) — ssot/MACHINE_AUTONOMY_LOOPS_v1.md §6, ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §7.

Truth order: live SSOT file beats repo state beats dispatch beats receipt beats chat; hidden state is a wall violation; one canon per domain extended by versioned pointer, never restated — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §9.

Session-exit law: any session mutating NOOS integrator state runs noos_integrator_sync_v1.py sync/session-exit before stopping; cloud/Supabase integrator writes blocked while cloud_owner.enabled=false; repo-local state authoritative — ssot/NOOS_INTEGRATOR_RULES_v1.md.

Least-knowledge prompt packets: workers get Tier 0 + their role layer + one ephemeral mission brief only; P0 CORE judgment text never enters worker prompts (soup-wall allows only goal, files, constraints, done_criteria, verify_method, receipts_required, decision_verdict) — ssot/AGENT_LAYERED_LAW_AND_LEAST_KNOWLEDGE_SSOT_LOCKED_v1.md, ssot/P0_CORE_DECISION_USE_CONTRACT_v1.md §6, data/founder_judgment_patterns_read_scope_v1.json.

Violation law: boundary violation → contain, receipt, critique, repair; repeat scope/authority violations retire the session/tool; never hide, rewrite history, or continue silently — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §10.
Intent filter Q1-Q5 applies to every proposed substrate rule/gate: founder-as-validator, permission loop, Sina-as-runtime, or frozen target → redesign or drop — ssot/FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md §3.
reusable_vocabulary: BLOCKED_WITH_REASON (universal refusal token), REJECTED receipt (second writer), canon_version: founder_canon_v1.0.0, receipt_id, work_order_id, dispatch line 'LAWS: FOUNDER_CANON v1 + governed-autorun v3. Violations = BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.'

Fence rule IDs: RF_RECEIPT_REQUIRED, RF_POLICY_LAW_MUTATION, RF1_package_status_active, RF2_operational_rollout_active, RF3_status_flip_from_audit_pending; checker name 'check_repo_fences_v1'; report statuses PASS/FAIL; exit codes 0/1/2; severity 'red'.

Policy YAML keys: policy_version, phase, protected_zones, exempt_paths, draft_branch_patterns, enforce_target_branches, policy_law_globs, audit_pending_statuses, receipt_reference_patterns, work_order_reference_patterns, fake_active_rules.
Status enums: ACTIVE, LOCKED, PROPOSED, Superseded, SCAFFOLD_READY_AUDIT_PENDING, AUDIT_PENDING, audit-pending.

Founder touchpoint IDs: FT-CAPITAL, FT-LEGAL, FT-L5-IRREVERSIBLE, FT-PHASE-UNLOCK; intent filter Q1-Q5; walls G1-G8; loops L1 WorkerExec / L2 MachineValid / L3 Adversarial / L4 SelfRepair / L5 OutsideAudit / L6 DeepResearch / L7 ReceiptProof / L8 EarnedAutonomy.

Lane enum: sg_governance | sourcea_brain | sourcea_build | trustfield_build | noos_doctrine | noetfield_website | assist_only; PR header fields lane:, motor_id:, motor_id_or_human_gate:, receipt_id:; 'task cell' and task_cell_owners; motor_id / owns / must_not_own registry fields; human gate id G6_sina_approve; B2 sign-off / SG_SIGNED.
Autonomy states: observe_only, promote_candidate, assist_only, registered_motor, phase_N → phase_N+1; expansion receipt receipts/earned-autonomy-expansion-*.json.

Claim ladder: VERIFIED / DECLARED / PLANNED; sender_declared; production_connected; risk_score; decision = route | build_automation | create_service_pattern; entity tags Noetfield / TrustField / SourceA / WitnessBC / SG / NOOS.

P0 CORE vocabulary: allowed_readers [base_live_brain, high_decision_agent], forbidden_readers (worker, tier_1_worker, build_lane_worker, ...), forbidden_tiers [tier_1, tier_2], dispatch_rule mode_gated_read_scope, stop_continue_verdict STOP|CONTINUE|ESCALATE_TO_SG, decision ESCALATE_TO_FOUNDER, harvest_proposed, dry_run, live_authority, receipt schema p0-core-decision-receipt-v1, task-spec fields goal/files/constraints/done_criteria/verify_method/receipts_required/decision_verdict.
Tier model: Tier 0 / Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3 (gates); 'least knowledge'; 'soup-wall'; agent id sg_sssot_cursor; owner team @Noetfield-Systems/sg-governance.

Naming conventions: SSOT law docs ssot/SCREAMING_SNAKE_v1.md, machine schemas data/snake_case_v1.json with top-level "schema" + "version" fields, gates scripts/verify_*_gate_v1.py, validators scripts/validate_*_v1.py, checkers scripts/check_*_v1.py, receipts receipts/<kebab-name>-<UTC timestamp>.json, draft branches draft/* | assist/* | copilot/*.
gaps: G1-G8 wall definitions are only a doctrine pointer: FOUNDER_CANON_v1.md cites 'WORK_ORDER_GOVERNED_IDE_LANE_v1 (G1-G8)' but no file in this repo enumerates G1-G8 individually — the substrate needs a machine-readable wall list (currently only the prose class list in canon §5).

policy/ contains exactly one file (repo_fences_v1.yaml). There is no policy file for cloud execution targets — no Cloudflare Workers / Railway / Supabase / GitHub Actions deploy gate, no allowlisted-command policy, no spend-cap policy; 'deploys' and 'token handling' walls exist as prose only.

No machine-enforced 'approval before external send' gate in SG: the TrustField human-approval rule and risk_score>=4 routing live as prose in ssot/sg-guardrails-trustfield-v1.md / sg-guardrails-signal-factory-v1.md with no verify script in scripts/.

Fences only fire at merge-to-main; draft/assist/copilot branches skip enforcement entirely, and there are no in-sandbox/worktree runtime gates in this repo — the substrate must add sandbox-boundary enforcement itself.

Fence checker validates receipt references by regex presence only; it never opens the receipt or checks receipt_required_fields (receipt_id, canon_version) — no receipt schema validator gate exists in CI.

Zone list duplication: protected zones are hardcoded in three places (policy/repo_fences_v1.yaml, the CODEOWNERS-coverage step inside .github/workflows/repo-fences-v1.yml, and .github/CODEOWNERS) with no single source; adding a substrate zone requires three synchronized edits.

Branch protection is not captured as code: CODEOWNERS comments say to pair with 'require review from Code Owners on main' and note the @Noetfield-Systems/sg-governance team may not exist yet in the org — actual GitHub branch-protection state is unverifiable from the repo.

data/p0_core_invocation_scope_v1.json is PROPOSED, not ACTIVE ('Do not register ACTIVE until SG review + receipt confirms') — the P0-PGR command-brain invocation scope the substrate depends on is not yet law.

No approval-language linter: canon §5 lists 'approval language' as a wall and PARALLEL_AUTOMATION forbids agent sign-offs, but no script scans diffs/receipts for forged approval or sign-off text (the 2026-07-03 forged-L5-sign-off incident motivated the rule).
No secrets/token-handling policy file or scanner in this repo (token handling wall is prose); substrate sandboxes touching CF/Railway/Supabase credentials have no committed gate.

Guardrail docs referenced from sg-guardrails-trustfield-v1.md live outside or partially outside this snapshot (e.g. mirror path ~/.cursor/skills/signal-factory/references/sg-guardrails-v1.md, canonical NOOS protocol in ~/Projects/noetfeld-os) — cross-repo mirrors are pointer-based with no sync checker for the guardrail mirrors themselves.

constraints: Phase 1 canonical routes only; no duplicate public route names; Phase 2 may replace routes only via explicit refactor doc — data/noos_control_desk_api_contract_v1.json ('rule') and packages/noos-control-desk-v1/NOOS_CONTROL_DESK_MVP_SPEC.md.
Control Desk binds 127.0.0.1 only; 'no cloud, no Electron, no Selenium, no fake audit' — packages/noos-control-desk-v1/NOOS_CONTROL_DESK_MVP_SPEC.md; enforced in control-desk/backend/server.py.
forbidden_until_audit: direct_main_write, cloud_deploy, electron_shell, selenium_automation, premium_models, fake_audited_registry — data/noos_control_desk_package_map_v1.json.

Pre-full-PASS cloud_write_scope_gate: only receipts, status, drift_reports, draft_branch_pr_prep may be written to cloud; fleet_rollout, active_promotion, direct_main_write, policy_law_mutation, audit_pending_registry_propagation blocked; cloud_write_unrestricted_allowed_before_full_pass = false — data/noos_copilot_dispatcher_v1.json and data/noos_control_desk_api_contract_v1.json ('integrator_gate').

Full PASS is not yet earned (pre_pass_gate 'full_PASS_not_yet_earned'); steps 8-9 BLOCKED until trustfield_copilot_cloud_agent resolves (22/23 PASS, 1 BLOCKED) — data/noos-copilot-dispatcher-mode-v1.json and data/noos_control_desk_package_map_v1.json.

NOOS Copilot forbidden_actions: promote_active, write_policy, amend_policy, propagate_audit_pending_registry_to_fleet, propagate_audit_pending_registry_to_cloud, fleet_rollout, direct_main_merge, policy_law_mutation, cloud_write_unrestricted, recurring_copilot_automation — data/noos_copilot_dispatcher_v1.json.

SG is pointer-only for integrator/sync behavior; NOOS (noetfeld-os) owns mode enforcement and runtime sync — docs/NOOS_COPILOT_DISPATCHER_AUTHORITY.md and data/noos-integrator-role-v1.json ('sg_does_not_own': 'integrator/sync behavior'). A substrate must not re-implement sync behavior inside SG.
Policy verdicts are computed backend-only; frontend displays, never computes — packages/noos-control-desk-v1/NOOS_CONTROL_DESK_MVP_SPEC.md.

GitHub Copilot UI is a bounded manual helper only (GPT-5 mini, Low, Manual; never autorun or architect) and is distinct from NOOS Copilot dispatcher — data/noos_control_desk_builder_roles_v1.json ('tool_law') and data/noos_copilot_dispatcher_v1.json ('distinction').

Cloudflare is deferred to Phase 3/4 control plane only — data/noos_control_desk_builder_roles_v1.json ('tool_law.cloudflare'); a v0 substrate targeting Cloudflare Workers conflicts with this law unless the phase is advanced.

P0 CORE may be invoked only by base_live_brain or high_decision_agent, never by workers/tier_1_worker/tier_2_prompt_bundle; requires at least one of the 6 invocation_triggers; no fabricated pattern match; no_pattern_11_without_ratification — data/p0_core_invocation_scope_v1.json and data/p0_core_decision_use_contract_v1.json ('hard_limits').

Output soup wall: P0 reasoning must not propagate into worker prompts; worker packets restricted to goal, files, constraints, done_criteria, verify_method, receipts_required, decision_verdict; gate script scripts/verify_p0_core_output_soup_wall_gate_v1.py — data/p0_core_decision_use_contract_v1.json ('output_soup_wall').

P0 CORE output is a decision proposal unless actor authority allows execution; it cannot directly mutate registry, code, gates, or prompts — data/p0_core_decision_use_contract_v1.json ('hard_limits').

Every P0 CORE invocation requires a receipt (receipt_required: true, schema p0-core-decision-receipt-v1, path receipts/p0-core-decision-<case_id>-<timestamp>.json) — data/p0_core_invocation_scope_v1.json.

Lock candidate submission requires all attestations PASS + checker PASS and produces bounded local branch+commit only, never direct main — data/noos_control_desk_api_contract_v1.json (/api/lock-candidate/submit) and builder_roles 'lock_candidate_step'.
reusable_vocabulary: Route names: GET /api/dashboard, GET /api/registry/load, POST /api/registry/save-draft, POST /api/attestation/save, POST /api/policy/check, GET /api/integrator/status, POST /api/integrator/sync, GET /api/receipts/list, GET /api/pr/prepare, POST /api/lock-candidate/submit

Role/actor IDs: noos_copilot_dispatcher, architect_spec_keeper, policy_checker_agent, backend_builder, frontend_builder, integrator_agent, noos_sync_mode_patch, repo_fences, workflow_audit_23, policy_check_step, lock_candidate_step, sg_records_result, negative_test_agent, critic_auditor, packager; P0 actors: base_live_brain, high_decision_agent; forbidden actors: worker, normal_worker, tier_1_worker, tier_2_prompt_bundle

Sync mode enum: read_status, publish_receipt, publish_status, publish_drift_report, prepare_draft_branch, prepare_pr (allowed) / fleet_rollout, active_promotion, direct_main_write, policy_law_mutation, publish_audit_pending_registry_as_active_fleet_truth (blocked); sync_model value 'mode_gated'

Gate field names: cloud_write_scope ('gated'), cloud_write_scope_gate, allowed_before_full_pass, blocked_before_full_pass, cloud_write_unrestricted_allowed_before_full_pass, pre_pass_gate, integrator_gate, gate_note

allowed_actions enum: integrator_status_read, integrator_sync_repo_local, receipt_write, status_publish, drift_report_publish, draft_branch_pr_prep, home_mirror_readout, cloud_mirror_readout_read_only

Invocation triggers enum: novel_case, lower_law_conflict, money_build_sell_sequencing, authority_registry_dispute, target_vs_blocker_dispute, no_tier1_rule_fits; verdict enum: STOP, CONTINUE, ESCALATE_TO_SG; escalation value ESCALATE_TO_FOUNDER
Worker task spec allowed keys (soup wall): goal, files, constraints, done_criteria, verify_method, receipts_required, decision_verdict

Layer names: P0 (package/contract authority), P0_CORE_CONTRACT (sub_layer), P2 (integrator/execution), P3 (mac_runtime local UI), P7 (governance_structure execution_contract), P8 (automation/policy), P99 (ledger/receipts)

Status vocabulary: ACTIVE, PROPOSED, RECORDED_STEP_5, SCAFFOLD_READY_AUDIT_PENDING, LOCKED_POLICY_ARTIFACT, COMPLETE_HONEST_BLOCKED, PARTIAL_EVIDENCE_RECEIVED, BLOCKED, ACCEPTED, RETIRED_BUILD_PHASE, pass_count/fail_count/blocked_count/todo_count

Schema naming convention: snake_case 'schema' field + '_v1' suffix (e.g. noos_control_desk_api_contract_v1, p0_core_invocation_scope_v1); artifact_id kebab-case + '-v1' (e.g. noos-copilot-dispatcher-v1, p0-core-decision-use-contract-v1); receipt filenames like receipts/sg-record-step5-noos-sync-mode-20260704T043000Z.json and pattern receipts/p0-core-decision-<case_id>-<timestamp>.json

Package member shape fields: artifact_id, path, layer, domain, stack_role, status, depends_on, affects_layers, operational_rollout_status; stack_role values: package_authority, master_law, copilot_sub_policy, control_ledger, machine_policy_config, policy_gate, local_ui_blueprint, attestation_contract, receipt_contract, cross_repo_sync_dispatcher, sync_mode_model, sync_law, execution_contract

Root repos list: sina-governance-ssot, noetfeld-os, SourceA, Noetfield, TrustField-Technologies; owner string 'SG (sina-governance-ssot)'; tool enum: cursor, copilot, noos_copilot, github_actions, cloudflare, control_desk_api, cursor_chat, noos

P0 contract field names: allowed_readers, forbidden_readers, invocation_triggers, input_schema (case_id, invoking_actor, decision_question, lower_tier_facts, conflicting_rules, available_receipts, proposed_options), output_schema (decision, matched_patterns, reasoning_summary, chosen_next_action, stop_continue_verdict, required_gate_or_receipt, harvest_proposed, harvest_note), no_match_handling, output_soup_wall, hard_limits, dry_run, live_authority
gaps: No order-intake surface exists anywhere in the Control Desk contract: none of the 10 canonical routes accepts a founder/client order, prompt packet, or job submission. A Cloud Factory Substrate needs a new order/dispatch route family added via the mandated Phase 2 'explicit refactor doc' path.

No cloud-reachable API: the Control Desk binds 127.0.0.1 only and cloud_deploy is in forbidden_until_audit. Client or Studio-IDE orders arriving over the network (Cloudflare Workers, Railway API, Supabase) have no existing entry point; only mode-gated cloud WRITES (receipts/status/drift/PR-prep) exist, no cloud READ/intake service.

No authentication/authorization model in the API contract — routes have no auth fields, tokens, or actor identity binding; multi-party (founder vs client) intake would need an identity layer that does not exist today.

No queue/packet bridge between the Control Desk and P0-PGR: grep found zero control-desk references in p0-pgr/*.json. The command brain (P0-PGR) and the dispatch surface (Control Desk) are currently unconnected subsystems.

No execution-target contracts for Railway or Supabase anywhere in these files; Cloudflare is explicitly deferred to Phase 3/4 'control plane only' in tool_law — the substrate's stated targets exceed current law.

Canonical integrator role behavior lives outside this repo (noetfeld-os/data/noos-integrator-role-v1.json, referenced as ~/Projects/noetfeld-os/...) and could not be verified from sina-governance-ssot; SG holds only a pointer (data/noos-integrator-role-v1.json).

Full PASS is not earned: step 7F-B (trustfield_copilot_cloud_agent) is PARTIAL_EVIDENCE_RECEIVED/BLOCKED, so steps 8 (policy check) and 9 (lock candidate) are BLOCKED — any substrate v0 must run entirely inside pre-PASS modes (receipts/status/drift/draft-branch/PR-prep) until this clears.

Attestation and receipt schemas are still PROPOSED not ACTIVE: packages/noos-control-desk-v1/.noos/copilot_attestation_schema_v1.json ('noos-copilot-attestation-schema-v1') and .noos/receipt_schema_v1.json ('noos-receipt-schema-v1') — substrate receipts would build on unratified contracts.

recurring_copilot_automation is a forbidden action and github_actions tool_law is 'model:none' — there is no lawful always-on LLM-driven cloud loop; a 24/7 factory substrate needs a new authority grant for recurring model-backed automation.

Spec/implementation drift: NOOS_CONTROL_DESK_MVP_SPEC.md declares FastAPI at control-desk/app.py, but the actual implementation is stdlib ThreadingHTTPServer at control-desk/backend/server.py + handler.py — the substrate design should treat handler.py as truth.

No sandbox/worktree vocabulary in any of these contracts — isolation primitives (cloud sandboxes, repo worktrees) for factory lines are entirely absent from the Control Desk and dispatcher law and would be net-new.

constraints: One actor · one repo · one scope; mixed-scope orders are defects; SG routes, it does NOT build venture workers (ssot/MULTI_REPO_WORKER_REGISTRY_v1.md).

One motor/writer per task cell; unregistered workflow or agent = defect on monthly audit; CAS on promotion gate — second concurrent writer gets a REJECTED receipt, never a silent overwrite (data/github_automation_registry_v1.json).

never_auto merge actions: write_to_venture_repo, mark_superseded_on_live_base, promote_without_receipt; manual_required includes founder_ratification, registry_row_promotion, cross_repo_copy_to_sourcea, phase_unlock (data/governance_repo_map_v1.json).
Version law: existing rules stay live; newer versions amend and win only on direct conflict; never label a live base rule 'superseded' (ssot/MULTI_REPO_WORKER_REGISTRY_v1.md §1).

Prompt registry: version immutable once activated; two versions never active at once; contract-test failure blocks only the new version (previous keeps running); cost_class 'dollars' requires founder-activated budget line via L8; founder approval required to activate prompts touching candidates, public copy, or spend (data/prompt_registry_schema_v1.json + L5 in data/cloud_line_registry_v1.json).
Phase-2 cloud-only rule: 'Mac executes nothing on schedule; every line writes a receipt; founder approvals are structural API gates, not conventions' (data/cloud_line_registry_v1.json).

P0-PGR cloud activation ladder: p0pgr-shadow-cycle-v1.yml is workflow_dispatch ONLY; the cron stays commented until the founder records FOUNDER-UNLOCK-R3-CRON-SHADOW receipt; shadow = compile+lint+route only, can never dispatch, deploy, send, or merge (ssot-p0pgr-workflow-bootstrap/.github/workflows/p0pgr-shadow-cycle-v1.yml).

Repo fences on main: changes under ssot/, data/, policy/, scripts/, .noos/, packages/noos-control-desk-v1/ require CODEOWNERS review + work_order_id and receipt references in PR body; RF1/RF2/RF3 block any status flip to ACTIVE while audit-pending (policy/repo_fences_v1.yaml, .github/workflows/repo-fences-v1.yml).

Copilot/coding agents are assist_only: draft PRs and suggestions only; must_not_own autonomous_promote, autonomous_deploy_prod, cross_repo_writes, external_send, unregistered_cron; required_pr_fields lane + motor_id_or_human_gate / receipt_id_or_explicit_human_approve (data/github_automation_registry_v1.json).
Brain artifact registration runs from SG only after independent verify — not from GH Actions (duplication_forbidden.brain_artifact_registration rule, data/github_automation_registry_v1.json).
L6 Deploy Truth drift → alert + HOLD flag row blocking L3/L4 autoruns until founder resolves; L1 never auto-rollbacks; L8 never auto-approves (data/cloud_line_registry_v1.json).

TrustField repo-policy.json lane limits: one lane per pass, max 40 files per pass, atomic commit per coherent lane; cross-repo dependencies only via contracts/exports/manifests/apis, never sibling-repo internal scripts or private runtime state.

Receipts must be emitted for every submit/sync/policy check with fields action, repo, files_changed, commands_run, policy_pass, errors, timestamp, next_machine_action; policy_verdict is server-computed, never accepted from the client (packages/noos-control-desk-v1/.noos/receipt_schema_v1.json, control-desk README).
reusable_vocabulary: motor_id values: gh_actions_brain_loop_autorun_v1, mac_launchd_brain_loop_autorun_v1, cf_loop_specialist_tick_v1, cf_auto_runtime_tick_v1, trustfield_loops_preview, gh_actions_sourcea_external_verify_v1, gh_actions_noos_factory_autorun_v1; motor 'kind' enum: github_actions, mac_launchd, cloudflare_cron, venture_worker, cursor_agent, github_copilot, github_agent.

agent_id values: sg_sssot_cursor, sourcea_brain, sourcea_worker, trustfield_worker, noos_agent, noetfield_website_agent, github_copilot_agent, github_coding_agent; lane values: sg_governance, sourcea_brain_observe_promote, sourcea_loop_specialist, sourcea_cloud_forge, trustfield_capture, trustfield_build, noos_doctrine, noos_gel_factory, noetfield_website, assist_only.

Registry field names: motor_id, kind, repo, workflow_file, cadence, lane, owns, must_not_own, requires_secrets, autonomous_promote, receipt_path, conflicts_with, required_pr_fields, task_cell_owners, duplication_forbidden, max_concurrent_writers.

Cloud line record fields: line_id (L1-L8), name, owner_role (noos_runtime, platform, sourcing_agent, evaluation_agent, architect, verifier, founder), trigger (schedule|event|event+schedule|schedule+dispatch), execution_target{primary,secondary}, input_source, output_artifact, receipt, failure_behavior, founder_approval_points.

execution_target values already in use: github_actions, cloudflare_deadman_worker, railway_api, github_actions_nightly, claude_cloud_scheduled_agent, github_actions_runner, railway_api_deterministic, github_actions_llm_narrative, github_registry_ci, railway_serving_prompt_runs, cloudflare_verify_recipes_worker, railway_asset_views_logging, railway_api_admin_dashboard, github_actions_telegram_digest.

Prompt registry vocabulary: prompt_id pattern ^(tb|pa|ops)\.<domain>\.<name>$ (e.g. tb.sourcing.r3, pa.eval.review-pack, ops.health.narrative); model_tier determine|fast|standard|judgment; cost_class zero|cents|dollars; status draft|testing|active|retired; run_trigger.kind cron|event|manual; owner architect|founder|machine.

Receipt vocabulary: ops_receipts.health_check / intake_summary / sourcing_run / evaluation_run / deploy_truth / asset_gate_audit / approval_decision / prompt_run; receipts/brain-loop-autorun-*.json; receipt fields action, repo, files_changed, commands_run, policy_pass, errors, timestamp, next_machine_action; verdicts PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED and PLATFORM_PACKAGE_PASS/PARTIAL; hash-chain columns payload_hash, prev_receipt_hash, chain_hash.

Supabase enums: host in (cloudflare, railway, github, supabase, traffic, mac); value_class in (REVENUE, GUARD, META, NONE); audit_status in (GREEN, YELLOW, RED); event_type in (loop_fired, loop_pass, loop_fail, probe, migration, deadman_alert); tables workflow_census_v1, workflow_census_runs_v1, trustfield_loop_registry, trustfield_loop_receipts, trustfield_verify_recipe_runs.

Naming conventions: lowercase_snake ids with _v1 suffix everywhere (files, schemas, motors, scripts); Cloudflare workers named tf-cf-<function>-v1 / sourcea-<function>-tick-v1 / sina-governance-ssot-advisory; workflows <topic>-v1.yml; Supabase migrations NNN_<name>_v1.sql.

Branch prefixes in live use: cursor/*, copilot/*, fix/*, ci/*, autorun/*, loops/* (loops/p0-cloud-factory-foundation), audit/update-registry-YYYY-MM-DD; fence-exempt draft lanes: draft/*, assist/*, copilot/*, */draft/*, *-draft, *-draft-*.

P0-PGR packet required fields: schema, packet_id, created_at, mission, problem_statement, deliverable, route, gates, roi, evidence_required, confidence, status (p0-pgr/P0_PROMPT_PACKET_SCHEMA_v1.json); founder unlock receipt naming: FOUNDER-UNLOCK-R3-CRON-SHADOW.

Infra identifiers: GitHub org Noetfield-Systems; Supabase project tkgpapowwplupyekpivy; Cloudflare accounts b7282b4a5c17b84d62e3ef8866b878f8 (SG) and 0d0b967b77e2e5535455d39ff3dae72c (TrustField); KV binding RECEIPTS; Railway URL trustfield-plan-worker-production.up.railway.app; CODEOWNERS team @Noetfield-Systems/sg-governance; sandbox env flags TF_WWW_UPGRADE_WORKTREE_SANDBOX, TF_CLOUD_LOOP_CONTAINER, TF_GHA_SECONDARY, TF_PRIMARY_MOTOR.
gaps: No sandbox manager exists anywhere: the only worktree code is TrustField's ensure_worktree_sandbox() — a single fixed detached worktree at .worktrees/www-upgrade-sandbox with no per-order allocation, no branch-per-packet creation, no cleanup/GC, and no registry of active sandboxes. The substrate needs an allocation scheme (e.g. .worktrees/<packet_id>/ + branch naming law) and a sandbox ledger.

No branch-naming law document; conventions (cursor/*, loops/*, autorun/*) are emergent from usage, only draft_branch_patterns in policy/repo_fences_v1.yaml are codified. Factory-line branches for the substrate would need a declared prefix (nothing reserves e.g. factory/* or sandbox/*).

data/prompt_registry_schema_v1.json is a record schema only — no registry instance (data/prompt_registry_v1.json) exists in the SG repo; the validated instance + CI live in TrustField-Technologies. SG-side prompt-packet storage/validation for the substrate must be added.

The lane enum in prompt_registry_schema_v1 (L1-L8) and cloud_line_registry_v1 lines are TrustField-scoped; there are no line_ids for the substrate's proposed lines (research, architecture, code, no-code app builder, workflow builder, test, deploy-preview, verifier, receipt, harvest) — the registry would need new rows or a parallel SG-scoped registry.

No artifact-storage convention for preview artifacts: today's options are GH Actions upload-artifact (www-upgrade-loop), the SG worker's Cloudflare KV RECEIPTS namespace, and Supabase receipt tables — none is designated for build/preview artifacts (no R2/S3 bucket, no artifact registry, no retention policy).

Railway is a live execution target (L2/L4/L5/L8 primary) but has no motor 'kind' in github_automation_registry_v1 and no registered motor entry; Supabase likewise appears only as a host enum value, not a registered motor — the substrate's registry additions must extend the kind enum or add rows.

Three divergent working copies of sina-governance-SSOT exist (/Users/sinakazemnezhad/Projects/sina-governance-ssot on branch cursor/machine-autonomy-loops-v1 with heavy uncommitted state, plus Desktop/Noetfield-Systems/sina-governance-SSOT and ssot-p0pgr-workflow-bootstrap); which checkout is authoritative for the substrate build is undeclared — e.g. p0pgr-shadow-cycle-v1.yml and the Supabase workflow-census migration exist only in the Desktop clones, while repo-fences and packages/ exist only in the Projects clone.

ssot/MULTI_REPO_WORKER_REGISTRY_v1.md (LOCKED) still points at pre-migration remotes (kazemnezhadsina144-dot/*) and paths (~/Desktop/trustfield-loops, ~/Projects/SourceA, ~/Projects/noetfeld-os) that no longer match the Noetfield-Systems org layout under ~/Desktop/Noetfield-Systems/ — the substrate's repo map should key off data/governance_repo_map_v1.json plus verified remotes, and the registry doc needs an amending append.

No secrets/credential registry: requires_secrets appears only on one motor (CF_MAIN_TOKEN, CF_VERIFIER_TOKEN); ADMIN_TOKEN, Telegram, Railway, and Supabase service keys are referenced in workflows/receipts but there is no SG-side inventory of which secret lives where for sandbox provisioning.
CODEOWNERS relies on team @Noetfield-Systems/sg-governance existing in the org ('Team must exist in Noetfield-Systems org; fallback owner review still applies') — existence not verifiable from disk.

docs/CLOUD_FACTORY_PROMOTION_PLAN_TRUSTFIELD_v1.md (authority for cloud_line_registry_v1) and docs/TRUSTFIELD_CLOUD_LOOPS_IMPLEMENTATION_PACKETS_v1.md exist in the working repo but are uncommitted; the substrate design should not assume they are merged law yet.

law_note: Drafters read ONLY files in narrowed_read_set (plus the binding spines and this query output). No repo exploration.
